claims.claim_text: The stated learning outcomes map to Bloom levels 3, 4, and 5 as Apply, Analyze, and Evaluate.
claims.span_hint: section: What You'll Be Able to Do, paragraph after the numbered outcomes
claims.rationale: The cited page directly lists the revised cognitive-order levels as Apply, Analyze, and Evaluate.

claims.claim_text: The 2020 RAG paper describes combining parametric model memory with non-parametric external memory.
claims.rationale: The arXiv paper abstract explicitly describes RAG in terms of parametric and non-parametric memory.

claims.claim_text: Anthropic's Building Effective Agents guidance distinguishes workflows from agents and recommends simpler workflow-style orchestration for many well-defined tasks.
claims.rationale: Anthropic's article directly defines workflows versus agents and says workflows are preferable for many well-defined tasks.

claims.claim_text: OpenAI's current tool guidance distinguishes hosted tools such as web search, file search, and code execution from developer-defined function tools, and advises documenting tool side effects and error modes.
claims.rationale: The exact current guidance is real and official, but the primary source lives on developers.openai.com, which is outside the current allowlist.

claims.claim_text: Kubernetes RBAC permissions are additive, with RoleBindings and ClusterRoleBindings granting the permissions they reference.
claims.rationale: The Kubernetes RBAC documentation explicitly states that permissions are purely additive and explains RoleBinding versus ClusterRoleBinding scope.

further_reading.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks
further_reading.why_relevant: Primary paper for the retrieval-versus-model-memory distinction used throughout the module.

further_reading.title: Building Effective Agents
further_reading.why_relevant: Direct source for the workflow-versus-agent framing and staged-control mindset.

further_reading.title: New tools for building agents
further_reading.why_relevant: OpenAI's main-domain overview of built-in tools, agent-building primitives, and custom-tool examples.

further_reading.title: Role Based Access Control Good Practices
further_reading.why_relevant: Concrete upstream guidance for least-privilege scoping and why broad generic cluster tools are risky.

notes: I excluded hypothetical scenarios, analogies, quiz prompts, exercise scaffolding, diagrams, and house-style design heuristics. The only clear off-allowlist inline-citation need is the OpenAI current-tools-guidance claim, because the exact side-effect and retry-safety wording is on developers.openai.com rather than an allowlisted host.
